dimensions.evidence: The production change exactly lowercases the input tagName before comparison, implementing case-insensitive lookup as specified. All acceptance criteria are covered: uppercase, mixed-case, lowercase lookups, nonexistent tags, and isTagSupportedByProvider parity are all tested.
Only the two files named in the plan were touched. tags.ts has a one-line diff; tags.test.ts adds only the planned test cases. No unrelated refactoring, formatting, or collateral changes.

The fix is genuine — it lowercases the input to match against lowercase tag definitions. No tests were deleted or weakened. The existing 'nonexistent' test remains, and new tests exercise uppercase/mixed-case inputs with real assertions on the returned definition object (not just toBeDefined). The cross-provider parity test verifies both casings return the same boolean.

Single-line change with no control-flow or type-safety regressions. Stored tag names are already lowercase, so the comparison is correct in both directions. No security, correctness, or regression risk apparent. The change is purely additive in behavior (uppercase/mixed-case now works) and backward-compatible.
